commits: 6b44c3d2f1b1ffcc52bfb917d0bfc9ddb7eb3290: test(0.8.20): RED — search_index_v2 leak at excise/rebuild/tokenizer + edge projector gap (R-20-E1)
e0e40da300453fcba3ed345e920e21b3d62cfaa2: feat(0.8.20): row-owned projection registry + total node/edge projectors (R-20-E1)
findings_addressed: D-B (design v5 §2) — modified `rebuild_shadow_state` (:6515), NOT `rebuild_projections` (:5949). v4's anchor would have patched the wrong function.

D-D (design v5 §2) — extracted `project_canonical_edge_row` from the inlined edge projection in `commit_batch` (verified at :12186-12220 at baseline, not v4's :12166). `prune_edge_projection_shadows` (:4127) only deletes and was left untouched.
§1 matrix — `search_index_v2` (content-storing FTS5, no `content=''`) is now deleted/rebuilt at ALL FIVE maintenance sites, not just WRITE + PURGE.

§1.1 refinement — registry models row-owned vs kind-owned. `_fathomdb_projection_state` is deliberately NOT registered (kind-keyed enqueue watermark); erasure never rewinds a kind's watermark. Confirmed at baseline that `last_enqueued_cursor` is write-only bookkeeping: `next_pending_projection_jobs` (:9375) and `database_has_pending_projection_work` (:9438) key off the projection cursor + `_fathomdb_projection_terminal` + `_fathomdb_vector_kinds`, never off `_fathomdb_projection_state`.
§3 Rule 1 — every erasure witness asserts on RAW TABLE CONTENTS via a reopened `rusqlite::Connection`; no test in this slice proves erasure via search results.

red_evidence.method: All four integration witnesses were written and run at baseline 19b568e2 BEFORE any src/ change; `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features operator --test erasure_projection_registry` exited 101 with 0 passed / 4 failed.
red_evidence.excise_source_clears_search_index_v2_raw:
panicked at tests/erasure_projection_registry.rs:131: assertion `left == right` failed: search_index_v2 residue for excised cursor 1: the erased body is still on disk
  left: 1
 right: 0
red_evidence.rebuild_repopulates_search_index_v2_raw: panicked at tests/erasure_projection_registry.rs:202: rebuild must drop the orphan search_index_v2 row: ["rebuild body beta", "rebuild body gamma", "STALEV2ORPHANTOKEN"]  (the injected orphan survived the rebuild AND the deleted legitimate row was never restored)
red_evidence.tokenizer_reproject_covers_v2:
panicked at tests/erasure_projection_registry.rs:268: assertion `left == right` failed: the tokenizer reproject must also re-tokenize search_index_v2
  left: 1
 right: 2  (control assertion on search_index passed in the same run, so the reproject did run)
red_evidence.rebuild_edge_projection_matches_write_pa:
panicked at tests/erasure_projection_registry.rs:379: assertion `left == right` failed: _fathomdb_projection_terminal diverged after rebuild: the rebuild path does not replay the write path's projector
  left: [(1, "up_to_date")]
 right: [(1, "up_to_date"), (2, "up_to_date")]  (the body-less structural edge's readiness terminal is truncated by the rebuild and never restored)
red_evidence.guard_row_owned_registry: NOT runtime-RED at baseline by construction: its subject (`ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS`) does not exist at 19b568e2, so at baseline it is a compile error (E0432/E0425, unresolved import `super::ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS`). Non-vacuity was instead proven POSITIVELY against the implemented code: de-registering `search_index_v2` from ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS makes the guard fail with `table \`search_index_v2\` is keyed by write_cursor but is neither registered in ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS nor listed as a non-projection source of truth.` The registry entry was restored afterwards.

blockers_encountered.description: While proving guard non-vacuity I ran `git checkout -- src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs` to undo the temporary de-registration. lib.rs was still UNCOMMITTED at that point, so the checkout discarded the entire implementation, not just the experiment.
blockers_encountered.resolution: Fully re-applied every edit verbatim from the session transcript and re-verified all five tests GREEN before committing. No work lost; the committed tree is the verified tree. Process note for future slices: commit (or stash) before running a destructive-revert experiment.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: `ProjectionPass` {Write, FtsOnly, VectorOnly} — one enum selects which half of the total projector a replay site is rebuilding. Required because the three replay sites differ: `commit_batch` needs both halves; the open-path tokenizer reproject must NOT perturb readiness/vector state; `rebuild_vec0` (include_fts = false) must NOT write FTS rows. Without it, 'call the projectors exclusively' would have changed rebuild_vec0/tokenizer semantics.

`canonical_node_rows` now also selects `canonical_nodes.row_kind` (+ private `row_kind_from_column`, unknown spelling degrades to Leaf) so the replay dispatches through the real per-row `index_targets_for_row_kind`. Behavior-neutral for FTS (all three row_kinds are fts:true), and more faithful than the previous rebuild, which ignored row_kind entirely.

`rebuild_shadow_state`'s edge replay query dropped its `body IS NOT NULL` filter (superseded + t_invalid recency filters PRESERVED verbatim) so body-less structural edges regain the `up_to_date` readiness terminal the write path records. Before this, a rebuild truncated `_fathomdb_projection_terminal` and never restored those rows, so `advance_projection_cursor` could not walk past a body-less edge.

Counting semantics deliberately preserved: `rows_rebuilt` still counts only FTS-projected rows (rebuild_report.rs asserts rows_rebuilt == 0 for a vec0-only rebuild). `rows_invalidated` / `projections_invalidated` now additionally count `search_index_v2` deletions — a strict increase; the only assertions on those fields are `> 0` (rebuild_report.rs) and field-presence (recovery_cli.rs), both still satisfied.

`ProjectionPass::VectorOnly` carries `#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "operator"), allow(dead_code))]` — only the operator rebuild seam constructs it, so the default recovery-clean build would otherwise warn.

commands: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets  -> CLIPPY_EXIT=0 (read via PIPESTATUS, not a trailing echo)

cargo clippy -p fathomdb-engine --all-targets --features operator -> 0 (the default-feature workspace gate SKIPS the operator-gated test targets, so this extra run is what actually compiles the new test target)

tests.cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features: exit 0 — full engine suite green, including the regression-sensitive targets rebuild_projections (3), rebuild_report, rebuild_vec0, excise_source (6), consolidate_provider (12), opp12_existence_axis (6), opp12_lifecycle_verbs (8), pr_g1_tokenizer_crash_recovery, f5_bm25f (3), exp_s_substrate (3)

eu7_status: NOT RUN in any form, at any N, on any backend (CLOSED BY HITL). `data/corpus-data/` was not symlinked. The `eu7_real_corpus_ac` target was COMPILED as part of the full engine suite and reported `0 passed; 0 failed; 0 filtered out` — i.e. it executed zero tests and measured nothing.
out_of_scope_untouched: No SourceId newtype, no erase_source SDK verb, no WAL/checkpoint work, no telemetry redaction, no migration (SCHEMA version unchanged) — sub-slices 5b/5c/5d.

src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json unchanged. The registry adds NO public Rust surface: ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS, RowOwnedProjection, ProjectionClass, ProjectionPass and both projectors are private; the guard test lives in the in-crate `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` precisely so the registry need not be exported.
dev/plans/**, dev/plans/runs/STATUS-0.8.20.md and dev/acceptance.md untouched (this output.json excepted). No push; primary checkout untouched.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Run codex §9 review on e0e40da3 (diff vs 19b568e2 = 2 files: fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs + tests/erasure_projection_registry.rs, plus the Cargo.toml [[test]] entry), then cherry-pick. Two judgement calls to put in front of the reviewer: (1) the edge replay now includes body-less edges so their readiness terminal is restored — confirm no consumer depended on those cursors staying non-terminal; (2) `projections_invalidated` / `rows_invalidated` now include search_index_v2 deletions, a user-visible count increase in the CLI excise/rebuild reports — flag for the changelog. Slice 5b (SourceId newtype / erase_source verb) can start from this head.
